short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $4.28B in FY2026 Q1, +2.7% YoY — decelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $46.10B (+977.1% vs current Q) — set by 29 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $2.65 vs current $2.82.
Margin context: 0.1% net margin — compressed — watch operating leverage.
Next earnings: 2026-07-22 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $17B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $182B → FY2 $188B (+3.3% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 14.0% — average operating profile over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +7.2% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Consumer Discretionary): Premiumization vs trade-down split — the long-run thesis depends on cohort mix.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 54.1% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 0.50 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).
30-day implied move: ±8.5% ($67.74 – $80.34) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -4.57pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $80.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Calendar spread at $74 strike: short 30 DTE / long 60 DTE. Mid IV, range-bound → harvest near-term theta while keeping back-month optionality.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 37.7% across 6 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 6 dates spanning 126–616 DTE.
398-day implied move: ±38.7% ($45.42 – $102.66) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $67 (-9.5% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-16.4pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $67 put / short $81 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
